description:
Goal
Land or discard the current dirty convergence batch before any new readiness/type-evidence work begins. The batch must prove that activation partitioning solves the observed quicksort/Enum.reduce over-specialization by improving the underlying facts, not by local fixture/runtime-helper patches.

Signal
- The worktree becomes clean through one coherent `fz-rh2.18.9.5` commit, or non-belonging edits are removed/split before that commit.
- Focused tests cover the retained root causes: callable identity preservation under widening, list cons non-empty construction, recursive return ascent, settled semantic closure reads, explicit artifact-rung demands, and CPS continuation back-edge marking if that native fix stays in this batch.
- `cargo test --lib compiler2::` passes under a hard timeout, and the real `fz2 --log-telemetry` quicksort build resolves within the agreed meaningful-event budget instead of producing runaway logs.
- Enum.reduce/range/list/fold-state-machine cases that this ticket claims are unignored and green.
- No debug prints, temporary fixtures, fixture-name special cases, qsort-specific logic, Enum.reduce-specific logic, or unchecked `fz2` run remains.

Strategy
- Treat every modified file as a candidate, not assumed-good state. Re-derive each change from telemetry and tests, then keep it only if it removes an old-world assumption.
- Preserve activation identity only where the callable target is semantically part of the value; otherwise let partition policy and `ActivationInputs` carry evidence.
- Move list construction into the type kernel so semantic lowering cannot locally approximate `[head | tail]` as possibly empty when the source form proves cons.
- Publish recursive activation returns by finite ascent, while non-recursive activations may still replace stale broad evidence with sharper evidence.
- Make closure/root/artifact tests demand the exact fact rung they prove; do not rely on opportunistic root submission walking later passes.
- Run the quicksort CLI telemetry path with `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout`; logs are signal, not something allowed to consume system memory.

Outro
No qsort/Enum/runtime-helper special cases, no local semantic list workaround, no closure identity erasure that loses callable targets, no recursive return oscillation, no pass-shaped artifact test assumptions, no hidden native stack-overflow regression, and no unbounded telemetry command.
title: Compiler2 convergence: burn down quicksort and Enum.reduce overspecialization
